entry.title: psytoolkittools 0.1.0-beta Release
entry.content:
I am excited to announce the first beta release of my psytoolkittools-R package! This version gives a first preview on the upcoming features and is meant for public testing. I am still working on some features and improvements, but I wanted to give you a sneak peek of what’s coming.

The psytoolkittools-R package is designed to help researchers analyze their data more efficiently. With this package, you can easily integrate survey and experiment data from you PsyToolkit experiments and surveys. Also, some other useful utilities are available such as to recode your key responses and statuses.

I encourage you to download the beta version and give it a try. Your feedback is important to me and will help me make the final release even better.

Thank you for your support!

psytoolkittools on GitHub
entry.summary: I am excited to announce the first beta release of my psytoolkittools-R package! This version gives a first preview on the upcoming features and is meant for public testing. I am still working on some features and improvements, but I wanted to give you a sneak peek of what’s coming.

entry.title: Introducing psytoolkittools: A new R package for PsyToolkit data analysis

link.title: Introducing psytoolkittools: A new R package for PsyToolkit data analysis

entry.content:
PsyToolkit is a free platform for online psychological research and information. It allows you to create and run cognitive-psychological experiments and surveys, including personality tests, in your browser.

However, if you want to analyze your PsyToolkit data in R, you might encounter some challenges. For example, how do you import survey and experiment data into R?

That’s why I am developing a new R package called psytoolkittools. This package aims to make it easy to interact and analyze PsyToolkit data in R. It is currently under development and not yet available on CRAN, but you can install it from GitHub using the following code:

# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("JonasEnCode/psytoolkittools")

I hope that psytoolkittools will be useful for PsyToolkit users who want to analyze their data in R. I am still working on improving the package and adding more features. If you have any feedback, suggestions, or questions, please feel free to open an issue on GitHub. I would love to hear from you.

Find out more on the psytoolkittools project site.
entry.summary: PsyToolkit is a free platform for online psychological research and information. It allows you to create and run cognitive-psychological experiments and surveys, including personality tests, in your browser. However, if you want to analyze your PsyToolkit data in R, you might encounter some challenges. For example, how do you import survey and experiment data into R? That’s why I am developing a new R package called psytoolkittools. This package aims to make it easy to interact and analyze PsyToolkit data in R. It is currently under development and not yet available on CRAN, but you can install it from GitHub using the following code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("JonasEnCode/psytoolkittools") I hope that psytoolkittools will be useful for PsyToolkit users who want to analyze their data in R. I am still working on improving the package and adding more features. If you have any feedback, suggestions, or questions, please feel free to open an issue on GitHub. I would love to hear from you. Find out more on the psytoolkittools project site.
